title: Silky Algerian Cacao Tagine
description: This delicious and silky Algerian cacao tagine is perfect for a solo dinner. It features fresh ingredients that are slow-cooked to perfection, making it both diabetic-friendly and easy to prepare.

ingredients.name: 8 oz boneless chicken thighs
ingredients.notes: Bone removed, skin left on for flavor

ingredients.name: 1 cup unsweetened cacao powder
ingredients.notes: Low fat or dairy-free options available

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup olive oil
ingredients.notes: For cooking the chicken and cacao mixture

instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Rinse the diced tomatoes under cold water.
Season chicken thighs with salt, pepper, and a pinch of saffron. Place in an oven-safe dish. Pour enough olive oil over the chicken so that it is lightly coated on all sides.
In another pan, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium-high heat. Add garlic and cacao powder to the pan and cook for about 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add honey, diced tomatoes, cilantro, and a pinch of salt. Stir well and bring to a simmer. Simmer for about 5-7 minutes or until the sauce thickens slightly.
Pour the mixture over the chicken thighs in the dish. Pour remaining olive oil evenly around the chickens.
Bake in preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the juices run clear when pierced with a fork.
Let it rest for about 10 minutes before serving.
Serve hot with crusty bread and garnish with additional cilantro leaves.
